matches.x: 0 itled--Commendeth ‘Timoth 8 solic — Com: imothy— eee Friendly admonitions. y OW concerning the. collection for the saints, as I have. given order 5 the churches at Galatia, even ao. do y' 2 Upon the first day of the week

Paul himself also alludes to it: "Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye. Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come." (1 Corinthians 16:1-2.) The Apostle John describes himself as being in the spirit on the "Lord's Day."…

Rabbi Jehudah said: If he be worthy she shall be an help meet for him; if not, she shall be against him to fight him.

When shall we all meet again?
    When shall we our rest obtain?
    When our pilgrimage be o'er--
    Parting sighs be known no more;
    When Mount Zion we regain,
    There may we all meet again,
NASB: Be on the alert, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.

It appears that Paul did not actually do the writing with his own hand, but dictated to a scribe, although he would sign the epistle himself. Hence we find in Rom. 16:22, "I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you"; and in 1 Cor. 16:21, "The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand"; and in 2 Thes. 3:17, "The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I…

Paul's statement, "If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maranatha" (1 Cor. 16:22), probably means, let him be accursed until the Lord comes." Maranatha, an Aramaic word meaning, O our Lord, come, appears to have been used by the primitive saints as a watchword or salutation by which they reminded each other of the promised second Coming. Finally, brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you
 all until His coming in His kingdom. Amen.
4 And this according to the grace of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, to whom be all glory, both now and forever. Amen.

if you have the Holy Ghost within you, and that this may be the case, is my prayer, in the name of Jesus. Amen.
